parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: Specifies the format of the response from the server, should be application/json.

model.description: The model used to generate the videos, default value is veo2-fast.

resolution.description: The resolution of the generated video, default value is 720p.

action.description: The action to generate the video. `text2video` generates from a prompt. `image2video` generates from 1-2 reference images (first frame, or first + last frames). `ingredients2video` generates from 1-3 reference images using the `veo31-fast-ingredients` model (the model parameter is forced internally for this action). `get1080p` is a deprecated alias for `POST /veo/upsample` with `action=1080p` and is kept for backward compatibility.

prompt.example: White ceramic coffee mug on glossy marble counter, top with morning window light. Camera slowly rotates 360 degrees around the mug, pausing briefly at the handle.
prompt.description: The prompt which used to generate the video.

video_id.description: The video_id need to refer to, this parameter is only valid when action is get1080p.

translation.description: Whether to enable the automatic translation of prompt words, the default value is false.

aspect_ratio.description: Set the video output size, this parameter is only valid when action is image2video, it can auto make the final video adapt to the aspect ratio.

image_urls.description: The reference image URLs for generating videos, this parameter is only valid when action is image2video

callback_url.description: The callback URL which will be called when the video is generated

id.description: The ID of the veo task.

request.description: The request of the veo task.

response.description: The response of the veo task.

parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: Specifies the format of the response from the server, should be application/json.

action.description: The upsample action. `1080p` upscales to 1080p resolution, `4k` upscales to 4K resolution, `gif` generates an animated GIF preview.

video_id.description: The id of a previously generated video task (returned by `/veo/videos`, `/veo/extend`, `/veo/reshoot` or `/veo/objects`).

callback_url.description: The callback URL which will be called when the video is upsampled.

parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: Specifies the format of the response from the server, should be application/json.

video_id.description: The id of a previously generated video task. The source video must not itself be the result of a `/veo/extend` call's offspring is allowed (an extend output can be extended further), but reshoot / object-edit outputs are also accepted.

model.description: The model used to extend the video. Only the Veo 3.1 series is supported.

prompt.description: Optional prompt that guides the extended section.
prompt.example: the camera slowly zooms out to reveal more of the landscape

callback_url.description: The callback URL which will be called when the video is extended.

data.description: The output video items generated from the prompt

parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: Specifies the format of the response from the server, should be application/json.

video_id.description: The id of a previously generated video task. Videos produced by `/veo/extend` cannot be used as the source.

motion_type.description: The camera motion to apply when re-rendering the video. `STATIONARY*` keeps the camera fixed (with optional pan / tilt / dolly-zoom). `UP` / `DOWN` move the camera vertically. `LEFT_TO_RIGHT` / `RIGHT_TO_LEFT` translate it horizontally. `FORWARD` / `BACKWARD` move it along the depth axis. `DOLLY_IN_ZOOM_OUT` / `DOLLY_OUT_ZOOM_IN` apply the classic dolly-zoom effect.

callback_url.description: The callback URL which will be called when the reshoot is complete.

data.description: The output video items generated from the prompt

parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: Specifies the format of the response from the server, should be application/json.

action.description: `insert` adds an object to the video. `remove` deletes an object from a specific area. For `insert`, `prompt` is required; `image_mask` is optional (if omitted, the AI auto-determines placement). For `remove`, `image_mask` is required.

video_id.description: The id of a previously generated video task. Videos produced by `/veo/extend` cannot be used as the source.

prompt.description: Required for `action=insert`: describes what to add. Optional for `action=remove`: describes what to remove (mostly for logging — the mask itself defines the region).
prompt.example: add a flying pig with black wings

image_mask.description: Mask image where white pixels indicate the region to operate on. Accepts either a publicly reachable HTTP(S) URL or a base64-encoded JPEG (with or without a `data:image/jpeg;base64,` prefix). Required for `action=remove`; optional for `action=insert`.

callback_url.description: The callback URL which will be called when the operation is complete.
